followupAnswerBank.question: What is the first thing you do when a production selection screen report crashes?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab ek production report crashes, first step short dump view karne ke liye transaction ST22 hai. Relevant dump find karne ke liye program name aur user se filter karo. Dump exact error type, woh ABAP line jahan occurred hua, call stack, aur crash ke time relevant variables ki values provide karta hai. Selection screen reports ke liye, common dump types include hain GETWA_NOT_ASSIGNED jab ek empty range table entry access karte ho, aur force-terminate messages ke liye MESSAGE_TYPE_X. Root cause samajhne ke baad, assess karo ki quick configuration fix ya emergency code transport chahiye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When a production report crashes, the first step is transaction ST22 to view the short dump. Filter by program name and user to find the relevant dump. The dump provides the exact error type, the ABAP line where it occurred, the call stack, and the values of relevant variables at the time of the crash. For selection screen reports, common dump types include GETWA_NOT_ASSIGNED when accessing an empty range table entry, and MESSAGE_TYPE_X for force-terminate messages. After understanding the root cause, assess whether a quick configuration fix or an emergency code transport is needed.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Production dump aaya — ST22 open kiya. Error: GETWA_NOT_ASSIGNED. Line 45: READ TABLE s_datum INDEX 1. Root cause: User ne date range nahi daala, s_datum empty tha, INDEX 1 access dump kiya. Fix: AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein s_datum IS INITIAL check add kiya. Emergency transport approved, imported. 30 minute resolution.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: SE38 mein directly code change karne ki koshish karna production mein — production system pe direct development generally locked hoti hai. Emergency transport ya OSS note process follow karna zaroori hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" ST22 common dump types for selection screen debugging:
" GETWA_NOT_ASSIGNED -> Empty range table access
" Fix:
AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  IF s_datum IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE 'Date selection is required' TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

" CONVT_NO_NUMBER -> Wrong value in numeric field
" Fix: Validate before conversion
AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  IF p_bukrs IS NOT INITIAL.
    IF p_bukrs CO '0123456789 '.
      " Valid numeric company code
    ELSE.
      MESSAGE 'Company code must be numeric' TYPE 'E'.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

" MESSAGE_TYPE_X -> Abnormal termination
" Check AT SELECTION-SCREEN for TYPE 'X' messages
" Replace with TYPE 'E' for recoverable errors

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you debug a background job that aborts due to selection screen issues?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ek aborted background job ke liye, SM37 open karo, program name se filter karo, aur failed job locate karo. Detailed error messages view karne ke liye Job Log button click karo. Selection screen-related aborts ke liye, common messages hain 'Required field is empty' indicating variant mein cover na hua ek OBLIGATORY field, ya AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein AUTHORITY-CHECK se authorization errors. Resolution: SE38 ya report selection screen se directly variant update karke saare required fields include karo, aur agar authorization errors present hain toh SUIM mein background user ki authorizations check karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: For an aborted background job, open SM37, filter by program name, and locate the failed job. Click the Job Log button to view the detailed error messages. For selection screen-related aborts, common messages are 'Required field is empty' indicating an OBLIGATORY field not covered in the variant, or authorization errors from AUTHORITY-CHECK in AT SELECTION-SCREEN. Resolution: update the variant to include all required fields via SE38 or directly from the report selection screen, and check the background user's authorizations in SUIM if authorization errors are present.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Background job abort raat 2 baje. SM37 check kiya — 'Required entry field P_BUKRS is empty.' Variant DAILY_RUN mein p_bukrs empty tha — someone had cleared it. SM36 se job opened, variant edited, p_bukrs 1000 add kiya, saved, job re-released. Morning run success. Post-mortem: Variant protection add kiya taaki unauthorized changes na ho.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Job log check kiye bina re-running the job hoping it will work — same error aayega. Pehle root cause fix karo, tab re-run karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Background job debugging workflow:

" Step 1: SM37 - Find failed job
" Filter: Program = ZSELSCREEN_REPORT, Status = Aborted
" Select job -> Job Log button

" Step 2: Read error message
" Common: 'Required entry field P_BUKRS is empty'
" -> Variant missing OBLIGATORY field

" Step 3: Fix variant
" SE38 -> Program -> Execute -> Fill all fields -> Save Variant
" Or: SE80 -> Variants -> Edit -> Add P_BUKRS value

" Step 4: In code - defensive background check
START-OF-SELECTION.
  IF sy-batch = 'X' AND p_bukrs IS INITIAL.
    " Extra safety - should not reach here if OBLIGATORY set
    WRITE: / 'ERROR: Company code missing in background run'.
    MESSAGE 'Background job: Company code required' TYPE 'A'.  " Abort
  ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle a situation where users report blank output from a selection screen report?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Blank output troubleshooting user dwara entered exact selection screen values collect karne se start karta hai — ek screenshot ideal hai. SE16N ya ek SE38 scratch program mein ek direct SELECT use karke un values ko database ke against verify karo. Common causes: date range inverted ya empty deta no matching records, relevant table mein data ke saath nahi company code, SELECT-OPTIONS exclude entries unintentionally saara data filter karte hue, ya authorization restrictions start of selection mein AUTHORITY-CHECK ke through silently results filter karte hue. Users ko self-diagnose mein help karne ke liye ek meaningful 'No data found' message add karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Blank output troubleshooting starts with collecting the exact selection screen values the user entered — a screenshot is ideal. Verify those values against the database using SE16N or a direct SELECT in SE38 scratch program. Common causes: date range inverted or empty giving no matching records, company code with no data in the relevant table, SELECT-OPTIONS exclude entries unintentionally filtering all data, or authorization restrictions silently filtering results via AUTHORITY-CHECK in the start of selection. Add a meaningful 'No data found' message to help users self-diagnose.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: User complaint: 'Report mein kuch nahi aa raha.' Screenshot mila — date range tha 01-Jan-2025 to 31-Dec-2024 (inverted). User ne galti se year 2024 aakhir mein type kiya tha To date mein. AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein date validation tha lekin message bahut generic tha — just 'Invalid date'. Message improve kiya: 'From date [date] is later than To date [date]'. Next time user self-corrected.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Blank output dekhkar assume karna ki yeh code bug hai — majority of blank output cases are user input errors. Pehle selection values verify karo SE16N se, code mein jaane se pehle.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Blank output debugging additions:
START-OF-SELECTION.
  " Meaningful no-data message
  SELECT vbeln erdat FROM vbak
    INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_data)
    WHERE bukrs = @p_bukrs AND erdat IN @s_datum.

  IF lt_data IS INITIAL.
    " Helpful message instead of blank screen
    MESSAGE |No sales orders found for company { p_bukrs }| &
            | between { s_datum-low DATE = USER }| &
            | and { s_datum-high DATE = USER }| TYPE 'I'.
  ELSE.
    WRITE: / lines( lt_data ), 'records found'.
  ENDIF.

" AT SELECTION-SCREEN - better date validation message:
AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  READ TABLE s_datum INDEX 1 INTO DATA(ls_r).
  IF ls_r-option = 'BT' AND ls_r-low > ls_r-high.
    MESSAGE |From date { ls_r-low DATE = USER } is after| &
            | To date { ls_r-high DATE = USER }| TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you communicate during an ongoing production escalation?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Production escalation ke dauran, regular intervals pe communication essential hai. Diagnosis se pehle bhi issue ko immediately acknowledge karo. 15 minutes ke andar ek preliminary update provide karo including kya information gathered hui hai. Estimated resolution time ke saath har 30 minutes mein ek progress update do. Resolve hone pe, cause batate hue, applied fix, aur kaise verify karna hai ke saath ek clear resolution message bhejo. 24 hours ke andar ek Root Cause Analysis document ke saath follow up karo cover karte hue kya hua, kyun hua, aur kya recurrence prevent karta hai. Yeh structured communication anxiety reduce karta hai aur professionalism demonstrate karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: During production escalation, communication at regular intervals is essential. Acknowledge the issue immediately — even before diagnosis. Provide a preliminary update within 15 minutes including what information has been gathered. Give a progress update every 30 minutes with estimated resolution time. When resolved, send a clear resolution message stating the cause, the fix applied, and how to verify. Follow up with a Root Cause Analysis document within 24 hours covering what happened, why it happened, and what prevents recurrence. This structured communication reduces anxiety and demonstrates professionalism.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Production escalation 10 AM pe aaya. 10:01 bola: 'On it.' 10:15 bola: 'ST22 check kar raha hoon — date range issue lagta hai.' 10:30 bola: 'Root cause found — AT SELECTION-SCREEN validation missing tha. Fix kar raha hoon emergency transport se.' 11:15 bola: 'Fixed. Please verify.' 12:00 pe RCA email bheja. Manager ne specifically communication style ko appreciate kiya performance review mein.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Resolve hone tak silence rakhna — management vacuum mein worst assume karta hai. Regular updates even when you have no major news is better than silence.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Communication template + code fix example:

" === ESCALATION LOG ===
" T+00: Issue reported - blank output for ZAGING_REPORT
" T+05: ST22 checked - no dump. SM37 checked - job OK.
"       User selection values collected via screenshot.
" T+15: Root cause identified - date range inverted by user.
"       No AT SELECTION-SCREEN validation for date range.
" T+30: Fix applied in development:

" CODE FIX:
AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  READ TABLE s_datum INDEX 1 INTO DATA(ls_dr).
  IF ls_dr IS NOT INITIAL AND ls_dr-option = 'BT'.
    IF ls_dr-low > ls_dr-high.
      MESSAGE |From date must be before To date| TYPE 'E'.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

" T+45: Emergency transport released
" T+60: Transport imported to Production by Basis
" T+65: Fix verified by user - RESOLVED
" === RCA to follow within 24hrs ===

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you triage multiple concurrent selection screen issues during escalation?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab multiple selection screen issues simultaneously arise hote hain, business impact se prioritize karo — month-end closing, payroll, ya customer-facing processes block karne wale issues pehle aate hain. Har issue ke liye symptoms aur affected users ke saath ek quick triage list create karo. Ek common root cause check karo jaise ek recent transport jo multiple related programs break kar sakta tha. Available hone par team members ko issues assign karo. Management ko communicate karo ki kaunsa issue pehle address ho raha hai aur reasoning kya hai. Rushed fixes avoid karo jo cascading failures create kar sakte hain — ek time pe ek careful fix.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When multiple selection screen issues arise simultaneously, prioritize by business impact — issues blocking month-end closing, payroll, or customer-facing processes come first. Create a quick triage list with symptoms and affected users for each issue. Check for a common root cause like a recent transport that may have broken multiple related programs. Assign issues to team members if available. Communicate to management which issue is being addressed first and the rationale. Avoid rushed fixes that may create cascading failures — one careful fix at a time.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Month-end pe 3 reports fail hue ek saath. Triage: Report A — payroll calculation — CRITICAL. Report B — vendor aging — HIGH. Report C — inventory reconciliation — MEDIUM. Common root cause: Transport DEVK900300 ne shared message class change ki. Message class fix kiya ek transport mein — all 3 reports fixed. Single root cause, single fix.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Multiple issues ko independently fix karne ki koshish karna jab common root cause ho — multiple fix attempts mein zyada time lagta hai aur risk zyada hota hai. Hamesha common root cause check karo pehle.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Triage framework for concurrent issues:
" Priority matrix:
" HIGH: Background jobs aborted for month-end reports
" MEDIUM: Interactive reports giving wrong output
" LOW: Cosmetic issues (field label wrong, extra whitespace)

" Common root cause check - shared objects:
" 1. Message class shared?
" 2. Common include program used by all failing reports?
" 3. Data element or domain changed?
" 4. Same transport imported that covers all failing programs?

" Quick shared include check:
REPORT zdiag_includes.
SELECT prog FROM D010INC
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_progs)
  WHERE incl = 'ZSS_COMMON_INC'.  " Shared include
LOOP AT lt_progs INTO DATA(ls_p).
  WRITE: / 'Program using shared include:', ls_p-prog.
ENDLOOP.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is an emergency transport and when is it used for selection screen fixes?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ek emergency transport ek change hai jo standard QA dwell time bypass karte hue transport path ke through fast-tracked hota hai. Yeh tab appropriate hai jab ek production issue critically business operations ko block kar raha ho — jaise month-end close, payroll, ya customer-facing transactions. Emergency transport ke requirements: management sign-off, documented root cause, basic development testing, identified rollback plan, immediate production import ke liye coordinated Basis team. Risk consciously accept hota hai. Resolution ke baad, ek post-incident review document karta hai kya hua aur ensure karta hai ki fix ko further refinement chahiye toh proper testing ho.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: An emergency transport is a change fast-tracked through the transport path bypassing the standard QA dwell time. It is appropriate when a production issue is critically blocking business operations — like month-end close, payroll, or customer-facing transactions. Requirements for emergency transport: management sign-off, documented root cause, basic development testing, rollback plan identified, Basis team coordinated for immediate production import. Risk is accepted consciously. After resolution, a post-incident review documents what happened and ensures proper testing is done if the fix needs further refinement.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Month-end closing tha, financial report dump kar rahi thi AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein. Normal transport 3 days ka process tha. Manager ne emergency approve kiya. Fix 1 ghante mein ready tha — basic testing kiya developer ne. Basis ne production import kiya. 90 minute total from issue to resolution. Post incident: QA properly tested, documentation updated.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Emergency transport ko routine process banana — 'Emergency hai nahi hai, jaldi chahiye' wala attitude. Emergency transport ka misuse change management process weak karta hai. Sirf real emergencies ke liye use karo.

" Actual fix applied:
AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  IF s_datum IS NOT INITIAL.  " Emergency fix - null guard added

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you prevent recurring selection screen production issues?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Selection screen ke liye recurring issue prevention include karta hai: har production incident ke baad clear preventive actions ke saath ek Root Cause Analysis likhna, selection screen quality gates (OBLIGATORY fields, date validation, F4 help, background variant test) ke liye ek developer checklist create karna, team ke code library mein standard validation templates add karna, release se pehle saare selection screen changes ke liye code review conduct karna, aur agar possible ho toh critical reports ke liye ECATT automated regression tests set up karna. Har incident kam se kam ek process improvement mein result hona chahiye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Recurring issue prevention for selection screen includes: writing a Root Cause Analysis after each production incident with clear preventive actions, creating a developer checklist for selection screen quality gates (OBLIGATORY fields, date validation, F4 help, background variant test), adding standard validation templates to the team's code library, conducting code review for all selection screen changes before release, and if possible setting up ECATT automated regression tests for critical reports. Each incident should result in at least one process improvement.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Ek team mein teen months mein 5 selection screen related production issues aaye. RCA ke baad: Developer checklist banaya — 8 mandatory checks before release. Code template library share kiya — copy-paste ke liye. Mandatory peer review add kiya for selection screen changes. Next 6 months mein zero selection screen related incidents.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle an escalation where the selection screen worked yesterday but not today?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab ek report kal kaam karta tha lekin aaj fail karta hai, key investigation question hai: kya changed? Overnight import hue koi transports ke liye STMS import log check karo. Recent changes ke liye SM20 security audit log ya change history check karo. Check karo agar yeh ek date-sensitive issue hai — new year ya fiscal period jahan hardcoded date defaults ab wrong hain. Check karo agar kisi user ka authorization overnight changed hua. Background job configuration changes ke liye SM37 check karo. 'Worked yesterday' constraint root cause ko recent changes ya time-dependent conditions tak narrow karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When a report worked yesterday but fails today, the key investigation question is: what changed? Check STMS import log for any transports imported overnight. Check SM20 security audit log or change history for recent changes. Check if it is a date-sensitive issue — new year or fiscal period where hardcoded date defaults are now wrong. Check if any user's authorization was changed overnight. Check SM37 for background job configuration changes. The 'worked yesterday' constraint narrows the root cause to recent changes or time-dependent conditions.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: January 1st — aging report stopped working. No transport changes. Investigation: Report had DEFAULT '2024' hardcoded for fiscal year filter. January 1st, 2025 — users expected 2025 data, report fetching 2024. Hardcoded DEFAULT was the culprit. Fix: INITIALIZATION mein sy-datum(4) se dynamic year. Lesson: Never hardcode year-dependent values.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Transport history check kiye bina code investigate karna — agar recent transport hai woh first suspect hai. STMS import log check first, code second.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" 'Worked yesterday' investigation checklist:

" Check 1: Recent transport imports
" STMS -> Import Overview -> Production -> Import History
" Filter: Last 24 hours

" Check 2: Date-sensitive hardcoded values
" WRONG - breaks on January 1:
" PARAMETERS: p_year TYPE gjahr DEFAULT '2024'.

" CORRECT - always current year:
PARAMETERS: p_year TYPE gjahr.
INITIALIZATION.
  p_year = sy-datum(4).  " Dynamic - always current year

" Check 3: Fiscal period change
" Select-options date default might reference previous year data
SELECT-OPTIONS: s_datum FOR sy-datum.
INITIALIZATION.
  " Dynamic last month range - works every month
  DATA(lv_last_month) = sy-datum(6).  " YYYYMM
  s_datum-low  = lv_last_month && '01'.
  s_datum-high = sy-datum.
  APPEND s_datum.

selection-screens-034.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: FS matlab Functional Specification aur TS matlab Technical Specification. Simple analogy: Ek building banana hai. Client ne ek document diya — 'Mujhe 3 bedroom, 2 bathroom, parking wala ghar chahiye, budget 50 lakh.' Yeh FS hai — kya chahiye, requirements. Architect ne usse technical drawing banaya — '30x40 plot, RCC construction, slab thickness 6 inch, column 12x12 inch' — yeh TS hai — kaise banana hai, technical details. ABAP mein: Functional consultant ne FS likha: 'Sales aging report chahiye. Input: Company code (mandatory), date range, customer optional. Output: Customer-wise outstanding amounts.' Developer ne yeh leke TS banaya: 'REPORT ZAGING_REPORT. PARAMETERS p_bukrs TYPE bukrs OBLIGATORY. SELECT-OPTIONS s_datum, s_kunnr. AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein validation. BSID table se data. ALV output.' FS se TS conversion matlab: Functional requirements ko technical implementation mein translate karna. Selection screen ke liye FS mein hota hai: Kaunse fields chahiye, kaunse mandatory, kya validations, kaunsa output format. TS mein hota hai: PARAMETERS ya SELECT-OPTIONS, data types, event implementations, database tables.
selection-screens-034.hinglishMasterExplanation:
FS to TS conversion for selection screen — detailed process:

Step 1: FS Read karo aur samjho.
FS mein typically yeh hota hai:
- Report purpose (kya karna hai report ko)
- Input fields (kaunse fields user fill karega)
- Mandatory/Optional classification
- Validation rules (kya check karna hai)
- Default values
- Output format (ALV, list, file)
- Special requirements (F4 help, dynamic fields, variants)

Step 2: Input fields ko ABAP statements mein convert karo.
FS: 'Company Code — Mandatory, single value'
TS: PARAMETERS p_bukrs TYPE bukrs OBLIGATORY.

FS: 'Posting Date — Range, mandatory, default: current month'
TS: SELECT-OPTIONS s_datum FOR sy-datum.
INITIALIZATION mein default current month.

FS: 'Customer Number — Optional, single or multiple'
TS: SELECT-OPTIONS s_kunnr FOR kna1-kunnr.

FS: 'Output Mode — ALV or List (radio button)'
TS: PARAMETERS p_alv RADIOBUTTON GROUP md1 DEFAULT 'X'.
     PARAMETERS p_list RADIOBUTTON GROUP md1.

Step 3: Validations ko AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein convert karo.
FS: 'Date range — From must be before To'
TS: AT SELECTION-SCREEN. IF s_datum-low > s_datum-high. MESSAGE TYPE 'E'.

FS: 'Company code must exist in T001'
TS: AT SELECTION-SCREEN. SELECT SINGLE FROM t001. IF sy-subrc <> 0. MESSAGE TYPE 'E'.

FS: 'Authorization check for company code'
TS: AT SELECTION-SCREEN. AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'.

Step 4: Dynamic behavior convert karo.
FS: 'Date fields hidden if date mode not selected'
TS: MODIF ID 'DT' + AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT + LOOP AT SCREEN.

Step 5: F4 help requirements.
FS: 'F4 help for plant field — search by plant name'
TS: AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR p_werks.

Step 6: Background scheduling requirements.
FS: 'Report must support month-end background scheduling'
TS: Variant design + sy-batch check + INITIALIZATION dynamic defaults.

Conversion mistakes to avoid:
FS mein 'mandatory' = OBLIGATORY for PARAMETERS, manual check for SELECT-OPTIONS.
FS mein 'range' = SELECT-OPTIONS, not PARAMETERS.
FS mein 'F4 help' = AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST ya search help linkage.
FS mein 'dynamic' = MODIF ID + AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
FS mein 'default current date' = INITIALIZATION with sy-datum.
selection-screens-034.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'FS to TS conversion ke liye main FS ko carefully read karta hoon aur har requirement ko ABAP components mein map karta hoon. Single mandatory fields PARAMETERS OBLIGATORY bante hain, range fields SELECT-OPTIONS. FS mein mentioned validations AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein implement hoti hain — date range check, master data existence check, authorization checks. Dynamic field show/hide requirements MODIF ID aur AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT se address hote hain. F4 help requirements search help linkage ya custom AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST se. Background scheduling requirements ke liye INITIALIZATION mein dynamic defaults aur variant design. TS mein main specifically likhta hoon: program name, all statement declarations, event implementations, database tables used, expected performance — complete technical blueprint jo senior developer ya fresher koi bhi code kar sake mere TS se.'

Hinglish tip: FS to TS mein 'traceability' ka concept important hai — TS ka har element FS ke kisi specific requirement se link hona chahiye. Interview mein yeh bolna: 'Main TS mein hamesha FS requirement ID reference karta hoon taaki traceability maintain ho' — yeh process maturity dikhata hai. Bahut developers directly code karna shuru karte hain — TS mein likhna alag level ki professionalism dikhata hai.
selection-screens-034.codeExamples:
REPORT zfs_to_ts_demo.
"*============================================*
"* TS: ZAGING_REPORT - Selection Screen Design
"* Based on FS: FS-AR-001 Accounts Receivable Aging
"*--------------------------------------------*
"* FS Req -> TS Implementation mapping:
"* FS-001: Company Code mandatory -> p_bukrs OBLIGATORY
"* FS-002: Posting Date range, default current month -> s_datum
"* FS-003: Customer optional, multiple -> s_kunnr SELECT-OPTIONS
"* FS-004: Output mode radio button -> p_alv/p_list
"* FS-005: Date validation FROM <= TO -> AT SEL-SCR check
"* FS-006: Company code existence -> AT SEL-SCR T001 check
"* FS-007: Customer F4 by name -> AT SEL-SCR VALUE-REQUEST
"* FS-008: Background scheduling variant -> sy-batch + variant
"*============================================*

" FS-004: Output mode
PARAMETERS: p_alv  RADIOBUTTON GROUP md1 DEFAULT 'X',
            p_list RADIOBUTTON GROUP md1.

" FS-001: Company Code mandatory
PARAMETERS: p_bukrs TYPE bukrs OBLIGATORY.

" FS-002 + FS-003: Date range and customer filter
SELECT-OPTIONS: s_datum FOR sy-datum,
                s_kunnr FOR kna1-kunnr.

" FS-002: Default current month
INITIALIZATION.
  DATA(lv_month_start) = |{ sy-datum(6) }01|.
  s_datum-sign = 'I'. s_datum-option = 'BT'.
  s_datum-low  = lv_month_start.
  s_datum-high = sy-datum.
  APPEND s_datum.

" FS-005 + FS-006: Validations
AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  IF s_datum-low > s_datum-high.
    MESSAGE 'From date must be before To date' TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.
  SELECT SINGLE bukrs FROM t001 INTO @DATA(lv_check)
    WHERE bukrs = @p_bukrs.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE |Company { p_bukrs } not found| TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.
selection-screens-034.interviewScriptEnglish: For FS-to-TS conversion I carefully read the Functional Spec and map every requirement to ABAP components. Single mandatory fields become PARAMETERS OBLIGATORY; range fields become SELECT-OPTIONS. Validations mentioned in the FS are implemented in AT SELECTION-SCREEN — date ranges, mandatory checks, and authorization. Custom F4 help is wired via F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST. Screen modifications go through MODIF ID + LOOP AT SCREEN. I add SE38 variants for typical user scenarios and document the screen layout and field-level logic in the TS so the developer and reviewer have a single source of truth.
selection-screens-034.interviewScriptHinglish: FS-to-TS conversion ke liye main Functional Spec carefully read karta hoon aur har requirement ko ABAP components mein map karta hoon. Single mandatory fields PARAMETERS OBLIGATORY bante hain; range fields SELECT-OPTIONS. FS mein mentioned validations AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein implement hoti hain — date ranges, mandatory checks, aur authorization. Custom F4 help F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST se wire hoti hai. Screen modifications MODIF ID + LOOP AT SCREEN se hoti hain. SE38 variants typical user scenarios ke liye add karta hoon aur TS mein screen layout aur field-level logic document karta hoon, taaki developer aur reviewer ke paas ek single source of truth ho.

selection-screens-034.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Converting a Functional Specification (FS) into a Technical Specification (TS) for a selection screen — explicit, traceable mapping.

Used for:
- Mapping FS fields to PARAMETERS/SELECT-OPTIONS with type, mandatory, default.
- Translating FS validations to AT SELECTION-SCREEN checks in proper order.
- Documenting MODIF ID, custom F4, variants and background scheduling from FS hints.

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- PARAMETERS ... OBLIGATORY, SELECT-OPTIONS ... FOR, MODIF ID, RADIOBUTTON GROUP.
- AT SELECTION-SCREEN (validation), INITIALIZATION (defaults), AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
- AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST + F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST.

Interview keyword: Traceability — every TS statement traces back to a numbered FS requirement.

followupAnswerBank.question: What does a Functional Specification typically contain for a selection screen?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Selection screen ke liye ek typical FS mein report ka business purpose, field descriptions ke saath ek input field list, mandatory/optional classification, expected data type ya reference field, default values, aur cross-field checks aur master data validations jaise validation rules contain hote hain. Output format requirements jaise ALV grid, list, ya file download specified hote hain. Specific fields pe F4 help, dynamic conditional fields, aur background scheduling compatibility jaise special requirements mention hote hain. FS ABAP syntax specify nahi karta — yeh business behavior describe karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A typical FS for a selection screen contains the report's business purpose, an input field list with field descriptions, mandatory/optional classification, expected data type or reference field, default values, and validation rules like cross-field checks and master data validations. Output format requirements like ALV grid, list, or file download are specified. Special requirements like F4 help on specific fields, dynamic conditional fields, and background scheduling compatibility are mentioned. The FS does not specify ABAP syntax — it describes business behavior.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: FS dekha ek project mein: Table with columns — Field Name | Description | Type | Mandatory | Default | Validation. Rows: Company Code | Buchungskreis | Alphanumeric 4 | Yes | None | Must exist in T001. Date From | Von Datum | Date | Yes | First of month | Must be <= Date To. Developer ne is table se directly ABAP statement list banaya. Smooth conversion.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: FS mein ambiguous requirements accept karna — 'Date range required' likha lekin FROM aur TO ka exact mandatory status nahi likhna. Ambiguity baad mein rework karta hai. FS review meeting mein sab clarify karo.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you convert FS mandatory field requirements to ABAP?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Mandatory PARAMETERS fields ke liye, OBLIGATORY keyword add karo — system automatically ek standard error message ke saath ise enforce karta hai. Mandatory SELECT-OPTIONS fields ke liye, OBLIGATORY directly apply nahi hota, isliye AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein ek manual check likhna padta hai: agar range table initial hai, ek MESSAGE TYPE 'E' issue karo. TS ko dono cases explicitly document karna chahiye common mistake avoid karne ke liye ki OBLIGATORY SELECT-OPTIONS pe kaam karta hai. Error message text meaningful hona chahiye aur field name reference karna chahiye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: For mandatory PARAMETERS fields, add the OBLIGATORY keyword — the system automatically enforces it with a standard error message. For mandatory SELECT-OPTIONS fields, OBLIGATORY does not apply directly, so a manual check is written in AT SELECTION-SCREEN: if the range table is initial, issue a MESSAGE TYPE 'E'. The TS should explicitly document both cases to avoid the common mistake of assuming OBLIGATORY works on SELECT-OPTIONS. The error message text should be meaningful and reference the field name.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: FS: 'Company Code and Posting Date both mandatory.' Developer ne: PARAMETERS p_bukrs OBLIGATORY — correct. SELECT-OPTIONS s_datum — forgot AT SELECTION-SCREEN check. QA mein user ne empty date execute kiya — no error, zero records. QA catch ho gaya, fix kiya. TS mein explicitly likha hona chahiye tha SELECT-OPTIONS mandatory validation separately.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: TS mein SELECT-OPTIONS mandatory implementation mention nahi karna — next developer assume karta hai OBLIGATORY lagega directly. Always explicitly document the AT SELECTION-SCREEN manual check requirement in TS.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" FS: Company Code - Mandatory single value
" FS: Posting Date - Mandatory range
" FS: Customer - Optional range

" TS Implementation:
PARAMETERS: p_bukrs TYPE bukrs OBLIGATORY.  " FS: Mandatory single - direct OBLIGATORY

SELECT-OPTIONS: s_datum FOR sy-datum,   " FS: Mandatory range
                s_kunnr FOR kna1-kunnr.  " FS: Optional range

AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  " FS: Mandatory range - manual check needed
  IF s_datum IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE 'Posting date selection is required' TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.
  " s_kunnr IS INITIAL -> allowed, it is optional per FS

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you convert FS validation rules to AT SELECTION-SCREEN?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: FS validation rules AT SELECTION-SCREEN checks pe ek specific order mein map hote hain: pehle basic field presence checks, phir format validations, phir master data existence checks (relevant tables se SELECT SINGLE), phir cross-field validations (plant company code ka belong karta hai), aur finally authorization checks. Har check ek clear message ke saath execution block karne ke liye MESSAGE TYPE 'E' use karta hai. TS ko har validation ko uske corresponding FS requirement ID, ABAP check logic, aur error message text ke saath document karna chahiye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: FS validation rules map to AT SELECTION-SCREEN checks in a specific order: first basic field presence checks, then format validations, then master data existence checks (SELECT SINGLE from relevant tables), then cross-field validations (plant belongs to company code), and finally authorization checks. Each check uses MESSAGE TYPE 'E' to block execution with a clear message. The TS should document each validation with its corresponding FS requirement ID, the ABAP check logic, and the error message text.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: FS mein 6 validation rules thi. TS mein AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein 6 IF blocks likhe — numbered comments se FS req ID mapped. Code review mein reviewer ne directly FS ke saath cross-check kiya — sab 6 validations implement hue the. Clean traceability.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Validation order galat rakhna — cross-field validation pehle karna aur mandatory check baad mein. Agar mandatory field empty hai aur cross-field check usse access karta hai toh runtime error possible. Pehle mandatory, phir cross-field.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" FS Validation rules -> AT SELECTION-SCREEN:

AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  " FS-VAL-001: Date range FROM <= TO
  IF s_datum-low IS NOT INITIAL AND s_datum-high IS NOT INITIAL.
    IF s_datum-low > s_datum-high.
      MESSAGE 'From date must be on or before To date' TYPE 'E'.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

  " FS-VAL-002: Company code must exist in T001
  SELECT SINGLE bukrs FROM t001 INTO @DATA(lv_b)
    WHERE bukrs = @p_bukrs.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE |Company code { p_bukrs } not found in system| TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

  " FS-VAL-003: Authorization for company code
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
    ID 'BUKRS' FIELD p_bukrs
    ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE |No authorization for company { p_bukrs }| TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle ambiguous FS requirements during TS writing?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab FS mein ambiguous requirements contain karte hain, TS writing ke dauran ambiguity ek clarification log mein document karo. Har item resolve karne ke liye functional consultant ke saath ek short meeting schedule karo. Selection screens ke liye common ambiguities: kya date range ko FROM aur TO dono require hote hain ya single-date allow karta hai, kya field ranges accept karta hai ya sirf individual values, exact default kya hona chahiye, aur kaunse specific table ke against validate karna hai. Saari clarifications FS ke addenda ke roop mein documented hone chahiye aur TS mein referenced hone chahiye ek clear audit trail create karne ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When FS contains ambiguous requirements, document the ambiguity in a clarification log during TS writing. Schedule a short meeting with the functional consultant to resolve each item. Common ambiguities for selection screens: whether a date range requires both FROM and TO or allows single-date, whether a field accepts ranges or only individual values, what the exact default should be, and which specific table to validate against. All clarifications should be documented as addenda to the FS and referenced in the TS to create a clear audit trail.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: FS mein likha tha: 'Plant filter — optional.' Developer ne SELECT-OPTIONS lagaya. Go-live ke baad functional ne kaha: 'Plant pe sirf ek value allowed hona chahiye.' FS mein 'optional' ka matlab single value tha — range nahi. Clarification pehle hoti toh PARAMETERS lagata. Rework hua. Lesson: Ambiguous words directly clarify karo FS review mein.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Ambiguity assume karke aage badhna — FS mein 'optional' ka matlab optional input ho sakta hai ya optional field ho sakta hai ya single value ho sakta hai. Clarify karo pehle.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Ambiguity resolution example:

" FS Original: 'Plant filter - optional'
" AMBIGUOUS: Does 'optional' mean:
"   a) User may or may not enter a value (optional field)
"   b) Single plant only (no range)
"   c) Multiple plants allowed (range)

" After clarification meeting:
" FC confirmed: Optional field, but single value only (PARAMETERS)

" TS UPDATED (after clarification):
" Original FS req: 'Plant filter - optional'
" Clarification date: 2024-01-20, FC: FC001
" Resolution: Single value, optional (no range needed)
" Implementation:
PARAMETERS: p_werks TYPE werks_d.  " Optional - no OBLIGATORY
" Not SELECT-OPTIONS - single value only per clarification

" TS clarification log entry:
"   Item: Plant filter - range or single?
"   Date: 2024-01-20  FC: FC001
"   Resolution: Single value only per business rule

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you document the TS for a selection screen for future developers?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ek selection screen ke liye well-documented TS include karta hai: ek header section report name, purpose, module, aur FS document reference ke saath; ek field mapping table har FS field ko uske ABAP implementation se type, mandatory status, aur default value ke saath mapped dikhata hai; INITIALIZATION, AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT, aur AT SELECTION-SCREEN ke liye event descriptions including logic aur FS validation references; validation ke liye use kiye gaye database tables; background scheduling ke liye expected variants; aur FS requirements se derived ek test case list. In-code comments TS sections ko reference karne chahiye future maintainability ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A well-documented TS for a selection screen includes: a header section with report name, purpose, module, and FS document reference; a field mapping table showing each FS field mapped to its ABAP implementation with type, mandatory status, and default value; event descriptions for INITIALIZATION, AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT, and AT SELECTION-SCREEN including the logic and FS validation references; database tables used for validation; expected variants for background scheduling; and a test case list derived from FS requirements. In-code comments should reference TS sections for future maintainability.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Team mein developer ne TS likha aur project pe aaya nahi 6 mahine. followupAnswerBank.question: How do you estimate effort for a selection screen based on FS?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: FS se selection screen ke liye effort estimation mein complexity ko component se categorize karna shamil hai. Standard OBLIGATORY ke saath simple PARAMETERS minimal time leta hai. Validations ke saath SELECT-OPTIONS per validation rule incremental effort add karta hai. MODIF ID use karke dynamic show/hide behavior planning aur testing time require karta hai. Per field custom F4 help testing sahit moderate effort add karta hai. Background variant design aur SM36 testing ek fixed overhead add karta hai. Ek useful baseline 0.5 days ek basic screen ke liye hai, plus 0.5 days per complex validation, plus 0.5 days per custom F4 help, plus background variant sahit complete testing ke liye 1 day.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Effort estimation for a selection screen from FS involves categorizing complexity by component. Simple PARAMETERS with standard OBLIGATORY take minimal time. SELECT-OPTIONS with validations add incremental effort per validation rule. Dynamic show/hide behavior using MODIF ID requires planning and testing time. Custom F4 help per field adds moderate effort including testing. Background variant design and SM36 testing adds a fixed overhead. A useful baseline is 0.5 days for a basic screen, plus 0.5 days per complex validation, plus 0.5 days per custom F4 help, plus 1 day for complete testing including background variant.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: FS estimation meeting: 3 fields — 1 day basic. 4 validations — 2 days. 2 custom F4 helps — 1 day. Dynamic screen — 1 day. Testing — 1 day. Total: 6 days. Actual: 5.5 days — estimation was accurate. Key: TS banao pehle estimation se pehle — TS se hi accurate complexity pata chalta hai.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: FS padhe bina estimation dena — common mistake. Developer bolega '2 days' bina samjhe ki FS mein 5 complex validations hain. Pehle TS draft karo, phir estimate do.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle FS changes after TS and coding has started?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab FS changes mid-development occur karte hain, response structured hona chahiye: change request acknowledge karo, already-completed TS aur code pe iska impact assess karo, rework effort estimate karo, functional consultant aur project manager ko timeline impact communicate karo, change ke saath TS update karo (include karo change date aur reason), code modify karo, aur saare affected scenarios re-test karo. Change history maintain karne ke liye TS document ko version karo. Yeh structured response scope creep ko silently untracked changes absorb karne se rokta hai aur project timeline transparency maintain karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When FS changes occur mid-development, the response should be structured: acknowledge the change request, assess its impact on already-completed TS and code, estimate rework effort, communicate the timeline impact to the functional consultant and project manager, update the TS with the change (include change date and reason), modify the code, and re-test all affected scenarios. Version the TS document to maintain a change history. This structured response prevents scope creep from silently absorbing untracked changes and maintains project timeline transparency.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Week 2 mein FS change aaya — plant field mandatory ho gaya (pehle optional tha). Impact: SELECT-OPTIONS s_werks already coded as optional. Change: AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein mandatory check add karna, existing variant update karna, test cases update karna. Effort estimate: 0.5 days. Communicated to PM. TS v2.0 updated with change note. Timeline adjusted.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: FS change silently absorb karna bina PM ko inform kiye — scope creep ki yahi shuruwat hoti hai. Har FS change ko formal change request mein track karo aur effort communicate karo.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you explain the TS you wrote to a junior developer who will code it?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Junior developer ko TS explaining business context se start karta hai — report kya karta hai aur kyun exist karta hai. Phir field mapping table ke through walk karo har field ka purpose explain karte hue, yeh mandatory ya optional kyun hai, aur kaunsa data type appropriate hai. Examples ke saath har event ka role explain karo: INITIALIZATION defaults set karta hai, AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT visibility control karta hai, AT SELECTION-SCREEN data validate karta hai. IF condition, MESSAGE text, aur expected behavior ke saath ek validation ka ek complete worked example provide karo. Questions encourage karo aur confidence build karne ke liye pehle few sections pe ek code walkthrough together karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Explaining TS to a junior developer starts with business context — what the report does and why it exists. Then walk through the field mapping table explaining each field's purpose, why it is mandatory or optional, and what data type is appropriate. Explain each event's role with examples: INITIALIZATION sets defaults, AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT controls visibility, AT SELECTION-SCREEN validates data. Provide one complete worked example of a validation with the IF condition, MESSAGE text, and expected behavior. Encourage questions and do a code walkthrough together on the first few sections to build confidence.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Junior developer ko TS samjhaya — 45 minute session. Whiteboard pe selection screen draw kiya. Fields list kiye. Events explain kiye with simple analogies. First two fields junior ne code kiya front of me — corrected immediately jab mistake ki. Rest khud kiya. Code review mein sirf minor comments the. Good TS + good handover = smooth delivery.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: TS email karke assume karna ki junior samajh gaya — documentation sirf written hoti hai, understanding ke liye verbal walkthrough zaroori hai especially for juniors.
